title: Chylismia eastwoodiae
intro: Chylismia eastwoodiae, also known as Eastwood's evening primrose, is a species of flowering plant in the evening primrose family (Onagraceae). This plant has been traditionally used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Chylismia eastwoodiae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chylismia eastwoodiae is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules to promote gut health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Chylismia eastwoodiae is a species of flowering plant in the family Onagraceae. It is a member of the genus Chylismia, which is a group of small-flowered evening primroses. The classification of Chylismia eastwoodiae places it within the order Myrtales and class Magnoliopsida. As a member of the Onagraceae family, Chylismia eastwoodiae is closely related to other evening primrose species. This classification suggests a connection to other flowering plants that have similar characteristics.
